input_args.parameters_json_dump: '{"abstract": "Spottail shiners ( Notropis hudsonius ) were monitored for eyeflukes monthly at four sites in the St. Lawrence River, Quebec, from spring through autumn in 1997 and 1998. In general, mean abundance of Diplostomumspp. in the lens of spottail shiners was highest at sites near large ring-billed gull ( Larus delawarensis ) colonies and was higher in 1998 than in 1997. Population studies demonstrated a major period of recruitment in the late summer \\u00e2\\u20ac\\u201c early autumn. Mean abundance increased between November and May, when sampling was logistically not possible, reflecting latefall or early-spring recruitment. Golden shiners ( Notemigonus crysoleucas ) and small yellow perch ( Perca flavescens ) were monitored at a single site (\\u00c3\\u017dle aux Ours) in 1997 and 1998. Mean abundance of Diplostomumspp. in the lens of golden shiners at \\u00c3\\u017dle aux Ours was higher in 1998 than in 1997. Recruitment occurred in the early summer and be tween November and May, suggesting late-fall or early-spring transmission. Mean abundance decreased between Au gust and October in both years. Yellow perch at \\u00c3\\u017dle aux Ours were infected with four different parasite species in their eyes. Mean abundance of Diplostomumspp. in the vitreous humour or retina increased during the summer and fall and then decreased between the fall and following spring. Diplostomumspp. in the lens increased in abundance in the early summer, and then decreased during the fall. Both Tylodelphys scheuringi and Neascusspp. in the vitreous humour were more abundant in 0+ than in 1+ perch. Mean abundance of T. scheuringiincreased over the summer in 1997 and then dropped to zero in the same cohort over winter, suggesting that the parasite lives for only 1 year. Neascusspp. only were found in 1998. Distributional data, together with seasonal information, suggest that habitat characteristics contribute significantly to the abundance of Diplostomumspp. along the St. Lawrence River. Proximity to gull colonies enhances abundance, but higher rates of predation at wetland sites depress abundance and restrict the accumulation of parasites. 1113 R\\u00c3\\u00a9sum\\u00c3\\u00a9: L\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122infection par la douve de l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122oeil chez le Queue \\u00c3\\u00a0 tache noire ( Notropis hudsonius ) a fait l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122objet d\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122un suivi mensuel du printemps \\u00c3\\u00a0 l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122automne 1997 et 1998, \\u00c3\\u00a0 quatre sites du fleuve Saint-Laurent, Qu\\u00c3\\u00a9bec. En g\\u00c3\\u00a9n\\u00c3\\u00a9ral, l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122abondance moyenne deDiplostomumspp. dans les cristallins des Queues \\u00c3\\u00a0 tache noire \\u00c3\\u00a9tait plus \\u00c3\\u00a9lev\\u00c3\\u00a9e aux sites localis\\u00c3\\u00a9s \\u00c3\\u00a0 proximit\\u00c3\\u00a9 des colonies de Go\\u00c3\\u00a9lands \\u00c3\\u00a0 bec cercl\\u00c3\\u00a9 ( Larus delawarensis ) et \\u00c3\\u00a9tait \\u00c3\\u00a9galement plus \\u00c3\\u00a9lev\\u00c3\\u00a9e en 1998 qu\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122en 1997. Les \\u00c3\\u00a9tudes de populations ont mis en \\u00c3\\u00a9vidence l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122existence d\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122une p\\u00c3\\u00a9riode de pointe pour le recrutement de Diplostomumspp., \\u00c3\\u00a0 la fin de l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122\\u00c3\\u00a9t\\u00c3\\u00a9 et au d\\u00c3\\u00a9but de l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122automne. Des Chattes de l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122Est ( No emigonus crysoleucas ) et des Perchaudes ( Perca flavescens ) de petites tailles, \\u00c3\\u00a9chantillonn\\u00c3\\u00a9s \\u00c3\\u00a0 un site (\\u00c3\\u00aele aux Ours), ont \\u00c3\\u00a9galement \\u00c3\\u00a9t\\u00c3\\u00a9 examin\\u00c3\\u00a9s en 1997 et 1998. Comme dans le cas de N. hudsonius , l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122abondance moyenne de Diplostomumspp. dans les cristallins des Chattes de l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122Est \\u00c3\\u00a0 l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122\\u00c3\\u00aele aux Ours \\u00c3\\u00a9tait plus \\u00c3\\u00a9lev\\u00c3\\u00a9e en 1998 qu\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122en 1997. Une premi\\u00c3\\u00a8re phase de recrutement du parasite s\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122est pro duite au d\\u00c3\\u00a9but de l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122\\u00c3\\u00a9t\\u00c3\\u00a9 et une deuxi\\u00c3\\u00a8me entre novembre et mai, ce qui semble indiquer une transmission automnale tar dive ou printani\\u00c3\\u00a8re h\\u00c3\\u00a2tive. On a observ\\u00c3\\u00a9 une diminution de l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122abondance moyenne entre ao\\u00c3\\u00bbt et octobre au cours des 2 ann\\u00c3\\u00a9es d\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122\\u00c3\\u00a9chantillonnage. Les yeux des perchaudes de l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122\\u00c3\\u00aele aux Ours \\u00c3\\u00a9taient infect\\u00c3\\u00a9s par quatre esp\\u00c3\\u00a8ces de parasites. L\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122abondance moyenne de Diplostomumspp. dans l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122humeur vitreuse ou la r\\u00c3\\u00a9tine des perchaudes a subi une augmentation au cours de l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122\\u00c3\\u00a9t\\u00c3\\u00a9 et de l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122automne pour ensuite d\\u00c3\\u00a9cro\\u00c3\\u00aetre entre la fin de l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122automne et le printemps suivant. Les Diplostomumspp. dans les cristallins ont augment\\u00c3\\u00a9 en abondance au d\\u00c3\\u00a9but de l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122\\u00c3\\u00a9t\\u00c3\\u00a9 pour ensuite diminuer au cours de l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122automne.Tylodelphys scheuringi et Neascusspp. dans l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122humeur vitreuse \\u00c3\\u00a9taient plus abondants chez les perchaudes 0+ par comparaison avec des poissons \\u00c3\\u00a2g\\u00c3\\u00a9s de 1 an. L\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122abondance moyenne de T. sch uringiau sein d\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122une cohorte de perchaudes s\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122est accrue au cours de l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122\\u00c3\\u00a9t\\u00c3\\u00a9 1997 pour ensuite tomber \\u00c3\\u00a0 z\\u00c3\\u00a9ro durant l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122hiver, ce qui indique que la long\\u00c3\\u00a9 vit\\u00c3\\u00a9 de ce parasite n\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122exc\\u00c3\\u00a8de pas 1 ann\\u00c3\\u00a9e. Des N ascusspp. furent observ\\u00c3\\u00a9s uniquement en 1998. Les donn\\u00c3\\u00a9es relatives \\u00c3\\u00a0 la distribution et aux fluctuations saisonni\\u00c3\\u00a8res de Diplostomumspp. sugg\\u00c3\\u00a8rent que les caract\\u00c3\\u00a9ristiques d\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122habitat exercent une influence significative sur l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122abondance de ce parasite chez les poissons du fleuve Saint-Laurent. Si la proximit\\u00c3\\u00a9 des colonies de go\\u00c3\\u00a9lands accro\\u00c3\\u00aet l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122abondance de la douve de l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122oeil, les taux de pr\\u00c3\\u00a9dation plus \\u00c3\\u00a9lev\\u00c3\\u00a9s des jeunes poissons dans les milieux humides pourraient contribuer \\u00c3\\u00a0 r\\u00c3\\u00a9duire l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122abondance et limiter l\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u2122accumulation de ce parasite.
